feed.title: ArXiv Query: search_query=cat:cs.CL OR cat:cs.AI OR cat:cs.LG

entry.title: Self-Correcting Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Verifier Feedback
entry.summary: We propose a retrieval-augmented generation framework in which a lightweight verifier scores each draft answer and triggers re-retrieval when confidence is low. On four QA benchmarks the method reduces hallucinated answers by 38% with a single extra mini-model call.

entry.title: AgentBench-Lite: Evaluating Tool-Use Agents under Budget Constraints
entry.summary: We introduce a benchmark for tool-use agents that measures task success under explicit token and latency budgets, and show that ranking of popular agent frameworks changes substantially once cost is included.

entry.title: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Training at Small Scale: A Practical Study
entry.summary: A systematic study of sparse MoE training recipes reproducible on a single 8-GPU node, including router stability tricks and an open training harness.

entry.title: A Survey of Memory Architectures for LLM Agents
entry.summary: We categorize memory designs for LLM agents into episodic, semantic and procedural stores, and map 60 recent systems onto this taxonomy with a discussion of open evaluation problems.

entry.title: Contrastive Reranking for Dense Retrieval
entry.summary: We train a reranker with contrastive pairs mined from retrieval failures, improving nDCG@10 by 4.2 points over cross-encoder baselines while halving inference cost.

entry.title: Understanding Grokking through Circuit Formation
entry.summary: We analyze delayed generalization in small transformers and link grokking onset to the formation of sparse computational circuits, offering a predictive early-stopping criterion.
